title: Elder Abuse: MedlinePlus
content: Elder Abuse On this page Basics Summary Start Here Learn More No links available See, Play and Learn No links available Research Clinical Trials Resources Find an Expert For You No links available Summary Many older people are victims of elder abuse. It is the mistreatment of an older person, usually by a caregiver. It can happen within the family. It can also happen in assisted living facilities or nursing homes. The mistreatment may be: Physical, sexual, or emotional abuse Neglect or abandonment Financial abuse - stealing of money or belongings Possible signs of elder abuse include unexplained bruises, burns, and injuries. There may also be bed sores and poor hygiene. The person may become withdrawn, agitated, and depressed. There may be a sudden change in the person's financial situation. Elder abuse will not stop on its own. Someone else needs to step in and help. If you think that an older person is in urgent danger, call 911. Otherwise, contact adult protective services. NIH: National Institute on Aging Start Here About Elder Abuse (Department of Justice) Also in Spanish Elder Abuse (National Institute on Aging) Also in Spanish Elder Abuse: How to Spot Warning Signs, Get Help, and Report Mistreatment (American Psychological Association) Preventing Elder Abuse and Neglect in Older Adults (AGS Health in Aging Foundation) Clinical Trials ClinicalTrials.gov: Elder Abuse (National Institutes of Health) Find an Expert Find the Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program in Your State (National Consumer Voice for Quality Long-Term Care) National Institute on Aging Also in Spanish Topic Image Related Health Topics Intimate Partner Violence National Institutes of Health The primary NIH organization for research on Elder Abuse is the National Institute on Aging Disclaimers MedlinePlus links to health information from the National Institutes of Health and other federal government agencies. MedlinePlus also links to health information from non-government Web sites. See our disclaimer about external links and our quality guidelines .
